Details: Removal of 17a-ethynyl estradiol from water by coagulation/clarification was found to be ineffective. Less than 20% removal was reported in three studies. For some studies, 20% removal was not considered significant considering the analytical errors of the experiments. No difference was shown based on the type of coagulant used for aluminum sulfate, ferric chloride or ferric sulfate.
